survivors.comment: Mutable default argument: `defaults={}` is evaluated once at function definition time and the same dict object is reused across every call that omits the argument. If this function is ever extended to write back into `defaults`, those writes will silently persist across call sites. The idiomatic fix is `defaults=None` with `if defaults is None: defaults = {}` at the top of the body.

File handle leak: `open(path)` is called without a context manager, so the file descriptor is never explicitly closed. CPython's reference-counting happens to close it promptly in the common case, but this is an implementation detail — PyPy and other runtimes do not guarantee it, and under load (e.g., many rapid calls before GC) this can exhaust OS file descriptors. Replace with `with open(path) as f: data = json.load(f)`.
